title: What is Evangelion about to you in a single word
display_title: What is Evangelion about to you in a single word
summary:
[]: What is Evangelion about to you, in a single word?             Quote Post by Th3Marauder » Mon Sep 09, 2013 12:56 pm For me, personally: Evangelion is about depression. What about you?

chunks.content:
Article: What is Evangelion about to you in a single word
[]: What is Evangelion about to you, in a single word?             Quote Post by Th3Marauder » Mon Sep 09, 2013 12:56 pm For me, personally: Evangelion is about depression. What about you?

[]:             Quote Post by J-kun » Mon Sep 09, 2013 1:46 pm For me, Evangelion is about acceptance.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Sep 09, 2013 2:59 pm This garden universe vibrates complete. Some we get a sound so sweet. Vibrations reach on up to become light, And then thru gamma, out of sight. Between the eyes and ears there lay, The sounds of colour and the light of a sigh. And to hear the sun, what a thing to believe. But it's all around if we could but perceive. To know ultra-violet, infra-red and X-rays, Beauty to find in so many ways. Two notes of the chord, that's our fluoroscope. But to reach the chord is our lifes hope. And to name the chord is important to some. So they give a word, and the word is... Mommy!

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Sep 10, 2013 12:29 pm Congratulations! Though seriously, EoTV = Acceptance -> Admittance; EoE = Forsaken -> Forsaking

[]:             Quote Post by kevinlowl » Tue Sep 10, 2013 1:43 pm hui43210 wrote: "Life"

[]:             Quote Post by J-kun » Tue Sep 10, 2013 4:57 pm riffraff11235 wrote: "Introspection"

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Tue Sep 10, 2013 5:25 pm Reflection. Although I wish I could elaborate more on what I mean.

[]:             Quote Post by Joy Evangelion » Tue Sep 10, 2013 9:41 pm Hallelujah

[]:             Quote Post by ShockTrance » Tue Sep 10, 2013 10:03 pm Imperfection. Specifically, the conflict between humanity's desire for perfection and the inherent imperfection of reality. Be it the control systems of the Evangelions, the mental conditions of the pilots, the automated defense systems of Tokyo-3, or even the very nature of the human soul, the world of NGE is overflowing with flaws, shortcomings, and a general incompleteness. Simultaneously, the ultimate goal of Seele is to drive the evolution of humanity out of its stagnate, flawed state to godhood (that is, perfection). ...I'm allowed to explain my reasoning for my single word, right?
